text.text:
Girlfriend and I went to twin flame. We did not have reservation so we waited in line starting at 4:50. Already a line had started to form. They stated they would only be able to accept 6 people over the course of a couple hours, with pairs of 2 every hour or so for walk in so if you want to walk in make sure you come at 4:30 pm and wait in line to make sure ur the first one…also try to keep it to a party of 2 if possible.

We were able to eat at 6:30 and the experience was worth the wait! I never experience the version in Japan but the meat was so tender and juicy, the rice tasted great, the service was great, nice to see the meat being cooked right in front of you. As mentioned in other reviews, the recommendation is to eat the first one alone, second one with sauces and third with an egg. My favorite sauces were the green chili (very spicy!) and the lemon yuzu (great sour taste!)

10/10 would recommend this place!

text.text:
it's a korean - japanese place so all of the side items are korean inspired and some of the hamburg toppings as well.
overall food was good but:

1. nice atmosphere although no music was playing until we were basically leaving. so would have been better vibes if they had some light stuff on during the meal.

2. the experience felt rushed. three hambagu's is a lot of food. while i was only a couple of bites into one, they drop off the next one, and it starts to cool down by the time you're ready for it.

3. they claim to binchotan but i noticed them using ogatan coals.

4. i also wish i had a japanese shoyu or salt lol. i wasn't a fan of their tamari.

our server matthew was hella chill though.

text.text:
This rating is strictly for food and vibes.

Twinflame just opened and you knooow I had to try it! I was able to snatch a last minute RSVP on OpenTable during their Grand Opening.

Twinflame is a Japanese-style robata grill, featuring hamburg steaks and korean sides, very similar to Hikiniku To Come in Japan. The vibes are very similar with it’s own style.

The space is very large and high-ceiling, a very modern industrial style. They have two charcoal grills with multiple chefs and cooks handling the hamburg steaks. 10/10 vibes for sure.

It’s mainly street parking - and I didn’t have trouble finding one at 5PM but I can see how it can get tough during prime dinner time. I recommend arriving early!

Now let’s get into it.

5/5 - Hamburg Steak ($35) - comes with three steaks, and IMO they are more flavorful than the ones in Japan! The sauces and seasoning on the side was okay, definitely prefer the ones in Japan cause they had a very refreshing radish side since the meal is pretty heavy.

5/5 - Gamja Jeon ($10) - crispy potato pancakes with truffle aioli sauce was super tasty. big fan!

4/5 - Matcha Ice Cream ($8) - great matcha flavor! not a huge fan of the wafers around it though, as it wasn’t really crispy and was lowkey giving styrofoam haha.

I was a bit surprised they didn’t include how to eat each hamburg steaks in the menu and solely relied on the server to inform guests… because my server didn’t inform me and I had to ask how to eat each one (just because I’ve been to Hikiniku To Come in Japan).

And it took awhile for my server to take my order, come check up on me, and give me the check. As someone who used to serve in high-pace restaurants, I was just confused on why the service was so slow, especially with limited seating areas and items on the menu.

As for the flow, customers who came after me received their order first but I ordered before them... and I then I received my order before another customer who came before me (cause we were chatting about that). I get that it is grand opening but there really wasn’t that many seats… and since my server barely checked up on me, I actually had to flag down other workers with questions, but it felt like they didn’t really know the menu either haha...

Overall, the experience was still good, and would definitely recommend friends to give it a try. I really do feel like they need to tighten up their staff.

I highly recommend making an RSVP on OpenTable, as their walk-ins are very limited and they don’t have many seats.

Shoutout to James though - he’s an amazing and sweet host!

text.text:
We had the chance to attend the soft opening of Twinflame, a new Korean-Japanese robata restaurant on Piedmont Avenue in Oakland, and it was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The food, atmosphere, and service all made a great first impression, and it's always exciting to discover a restaurant that gets so many things right from day one.

We started with the Doobu Kimchi, a dish made with crispy organic fried tofu, sautéed kimchi, kimchi purée, crispy nori, and scallions. It's vegan and gluten-free, but more importantly, it's packed with flavor and a great way to begin the meal.

For drinks, I enjoyed a glass of Domaine Red, Les Garçons Brouilly from France, followed by a glass of Shichida Junmai sake. Both paired beautifully with the food.

The highlight of the evening was the Japanese-style Wagyu hamburg steak, grilled over binchotan charcoal and served with Koshihikari rice, miso soup, and a perfectly poached egg. Rich, smoky, comforting, and easily one of my favorite dishes of the night.

We finished with ceremonial matcha ice cream served in a crisp monaka shell. It was creamy, balanced, and the perfect ending to an excellent dinner.

text.text:
5 out of 5 for this soft opening! While it was funny to me to be Korean and having a white guy explain that the appetizers were all Korean dishes, it did not detract from the great service, perfectly cooked beef, and overall nice atmosphere.

We arrived 5mins early and were sat at the bar to wait for our table with apologies they couldn’t seat us faster. About 7mins pass and they seat us so not a wait at all really.

We ordered one hamburg steak set to share with an extra steak and two appetizers with matcha ice cream to finish.

Can’t wait to see how they evolve.
